title: eclipta alba
intro: Eclipta alba, also known as false daisy or bhringaraj, is a herbaceous annual plant native to North America and Asia, characterized by its daisy-like flowers and medicinal properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, which are thought to be due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. Eclipta alba is traditionally used to promote hair growth, improve cognitive function, and support the immune system, among other health benefits. In herbal preparations, Eclipta alba is often used as a hair tonic, added to teas or infusions, or used as a topical cream to enhance wound healing and reduce inflammation.
